title: EIP-7851: Deactivate a Delegated EOA's Key
description: Introduces a system contract enabling EIP-7702 delegated EOAs to irreversibly deactivate their ECDSA private key for transaction sending and authorizations. Deactivation uses a 7-day cancellation window before finalization. After the delay, the key is permanently deactivated in-protocol. The system contract serves as the single source of truth for key status.
author: Liyi Guo (@colinlyguo), Nicolas Consigny (@nconsigny)
laymanDescription: Lets users who have upgraded their Ethereum account to a smart contract wallet permanently turn off their original private key after a 7-day safety window. This prevents anyone, including a future quantum computer, from using the old key to override the smart contract wallet.

endUsers.description: Users with EIP-7702 delegated accounts gain an opt-in path to permanently disable their ECDSA key, strengthening account security against key compromise and quantum threats.

appDevs.description: Contracts using ECDSA signatures (e.g., permit) should check deactivation status via the system contract; non-upgradeable contracts may need a companion ecRecover change.

walletDevs.description: Wallets supporting EIP-7702 accounts must surface the deactivation flow, 7-day delay status, and cancellation option, with clear warnings about irreversibility.

toolingInfra.description: Indexers and explorers need to track the system contract's deactivation state and display key status (active, pending, deactivated, dormant) for delegated EOAs.

layer2s.description: No direct impact unless L2s independently adopt the same deactivation mechanism; existing L2 operations are unaffected.

stakersNodes.description: No direct impact on validators or staking operations; the change applies only to EOA transaction validation, not consensus.

elClients.description: Must deploy the system contract at fork activation, add a storage read during transaction validation for delegated senders, and increase PER_AUTH_BASE_COST by 2100 gas.

clClients.description: No consensus-layer changes required; this EIP operates entirely within the execution layer via a system contract and transaction validation rules.

benefits: Provides a one-way migration path from EOA to full smart contract wallet control.
Strengthens post-quantum security by permanently disabling ECDSA signing authority.
7-day cancellation window protects against accidental or unauthorized deactivation.
Uses the established system contract pattern, avoiding account state or precompile complexity.
tradeoffs: Deactivation is irreversible; users who lose access to their delegate contract lose the account permanently.
Adds a storage read per delegated sender during transaction validation, increasing gas costs.

improveUX.description: Enables EOA users to fully transition to smart contract wallets with confidence that the original key cannot override wallet logic.
